sample.title: How major US stock indexes fared Tuesday 5/26/2026
sample.memo: The U.S. stock market rose to more all-time highs on Tuesday after trading resumed following Monday's holiday. The S&P 500 rose 45.65 points, or 0.6%, to 7,519.12. The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 118.02 points, or 0.2%, to 50,461.68. The Nasdaq Composite rose 312.21 points, or 1.2%, to 26,656.18. The Russell 2000 rose 51.31 points, or 1.8%, to 2,920.54. For the year, the S&P 500 is up 673.62 points, or 9.8%, the Dow is up 2,398.39 points, or 5%, the Nasdaq is up 3,414.19 points, or 14.7%, and the Russell 2000 is up 438.63 points, or 17.7%.

sample.title: How major US stock indexes fared Wednesday 5/27/2026
sample.memo: U.S. stocks inched to more records Wednesday. The S&P 500 rose 1.24 points, or less than 0.1%, to 7,520.36. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 182.60 points, or 0.4%, to 50,644.28. The Nasdaq Composite rose 18.55 points, or 0.1%, to 26,674.73. The Russell 2000 fell 0.60 points, or less than 0.1%, to 2,919.94. For the week, the S&P 500 is up 46.89 points, or 0.6%, while the Nasdaq is up 330.76 points, or 1.3%.

sample.title: Wall Street pushes to more records as profits keep piling up for US companies
sample.memo: Wall Street pushed toward more records Thursday as corporate profits stayed strong. The S&P 500 added 0.4% in late morning trading. Snowflake rose 34.1% after profit and revenue exceeded expectations. Dollar Tree rose 18.1%, Kohl's rallied 16.3%, Best Buy climbed 15.9%, and Hormel Foods climbed 9.8% after stronger-than-expected profit reports. Marvell Technology fell 1.3% after its profit matched expectations.

sample.title: NVIDIA Announces Financial Results for First Quarter Fiscal 2027
sample.memo: Nvidia reported record revenue of $81.6 billion for the first quarter ended April 26, 2026, up 20% from the previous quarter and up 85% from a year earlier. GAAP diluted EPS was $2.39, while non-GAAP diluted EPS was $1.87. The company also said AI infrastructure demand remained strong.

sample.title: Apple Reports Record-Breaking 2Q 2026 Results

sample.title: Amazon.com Announces First Quarter Results
sample.memo: Amazon reported first-quarter net sales of $181.5 billion, up 17% from a year earlier. AWS segment sales increased 28% year over year to $37.6 billion. Operating income increased to $23.9 billion from $18.4 billion in the prior-year quarter. Management said AWS had its fastest growth in 15 quarters.
